domain_questions.question: When something genuinely good finally happens to you, what is your honest reaction?

options.A: I start organizing it — already thinking about how to make the most of it.
options.B: It bursts out of me. I can't keep it contained and I don't try.
options.C: A jolt of energy — I want to be up and moving.
options.D: A quiet, settled feeling, like something heavy clicked into place.
options.E: I look around at everyone else, reading whether they feel it too.
options.F: I keep it to myself for a while before I let anyone know.
options.G: I turn it over inwardly, savoring exactly what it means before anything else.
options.H: A kind of clarity — like a light switched on and I can finally see the whole shape of things.

domain_questions.question: Someone insults you in a way that actually stings. Underneath, what happens?

options.A: I plant my feet. I'm not going anywhere and I'm not going to crumble.
options.B: A hot flare of anger I have to decide whether to let out.
options.C: I step back mentally and start analyzing why they said it.
options.D: I'm already three comebacks ahead, talking before I've thought.
options.E: I go still and quiet and give them nothing to read.
options.F: I shift my whole manner to throw them off balance.
options.G: I read what's really driving them and answer that, not the insult.
options.H: I become very still and watch them, letting them perform while I see through it.

domain_questions.question: What do you trust most about yourself when things get hard?

options.A: That I can quietly remove myself from a bad situation.
options.B: That when I finally commit, I commit completely.
options.C: That I can take a beating and still be standing.
options.D: That I'll instinctively find what the moment needs.
options.E: That I can keep a clear head and manage the chaos.
options.F: That I'll spot the opening before anyone else does.
options.G: That my mind stays mine — clear, sealed, and under my own control.
options.H: That I can read what's really happening even when everyone else is blinded by it.

domain_questions.question: How do you most like to occupy a space — a party, an office, a room?

options.A: I read the room and blend into whatever it needs.
options.B: I bring the energy; the mood tends to follow me.
options.C: I'm a steady, solid presence people can lean on.
options.D: I like a vantage point where I can see and steer everything.
options.E: I move around constantly; I hate being pinned in one spot.
options.F: I prefer the edges, where I can watch without being watched.
options.G: I tune into what everyone's actually thinking and move through that.
options.H: I find the line between light and dark and stand there, watching both.

domain_questions.question: Which kind of flexibility are you proudest of in yourself?

options.A: How fast I can change direction when plans fall apart.
options.B: How much I can take before I bend at all.
options.C: How well I can hold many moving parts in my head at once.
options.D: How completely I can throw myself into one thing.
options.E: How easily I take on whatever a situation asks of me.
options.F: How I can slip out of commitments that no longer serve me.
options.G: How completely I can keep my own head no matter the noise.
options.H: How I can shift between being utterly visible and completely unseen without losing my center.

domain_questions.question: Once you've truly committed to something, which word fits you best?

options.A: Immovable — nothing talks me out of it.
options.B: Adaptive — I become whatever finishing it requires.
options.C: Discreet — I'd rather no one know it was me.
options.D: Quick — I want it done before the moment passes.
options.E: Intense — I go all in, full burn.
options.F: Deliberate — I steer the whole thing from the background.
options.G: Penetrating — I get to the truth of a thing and won't be fooled out of it.
options.H: Luminous — when I commit, I become impossible to ignore, or I vanish entirely; either way, it's total.

domain_questions.question: Where does your sense of self-worth honestly come from?

options.A: Carrying loads other people can't.
options.B: Knowing I can stay self-contained and need no one.
options.C: Being impossible to ignore when I show up.
options.D: Being the one who keeps everything organized and running.
options.E: Fitting in and thriving anywhere I'm dropped.
options.F: Being faster off the mark than everyone around me.
options.G: Understanding people more deeply than they understand themselves.
options.H: Being the one who sees what others miss, in the light or the dark.

domain_questions.question: Which secret wish is most you?

options.A: To handle a dozen things at once without dropping any.
options.B: To go anywhere I want the instant I want to.
options.C: To slip into places unseen and leave no trace.
options.D: To be so solid that no problem could move me.
options.E: To make the natural world feel like an extension of me.
options.F: To unleash everything I've got, all at once, just once.
options.G: To know what's truly going on inside the people around me.
options.H: To be both the light that reveals and the shadow that conceals, as I choose.

domain_questions.question: How do you most naturally reach another person?

options.A: By matching or shifting their emotional temperature.
options.B: By being a dependable rock they can hold onto.
options.C: By thinking through what they need and arranging it.
options.D: By closing the gap fast, before the moment cools.

options.F: By tuning myself to their mood until we're in sync.
options.G: By sensing what they really mean beneath what they say.
options.H: By showing them what they keep in shadow — gently, so they can finally see it.

domain_questions.question: When you decide you're going to win, what's your honest first move?

options.A: Hit hard and early, with everything I've got.
options.B: Feel out the opponent and adapt to what they show me.
options.C: Get there first; speed is my weapon.
options.D: Stay unseen and pick my moment.
options.E: Take control of the variables and arrange them my way.
options.F: Meet them head on and simply outlast and overpower them.
options.G: Get inside their head and turn their own intentions against them.
options.H: Strike from where they aren't looking, with a precision that arrives like fate.

domain_questions.question: What kind of resilience best describes you?

options.A: I bounce off and keep moving; I don't dwell.
options.B: I step back, reassess, and re-plan around the hit.
options.C: I absorb it and don't break.
options.D: I withdraw, regroup quietly, and re-emerge later.

options.F: I change myself to survive what's coming.
options.G: I shield my mind, stay clear, and refuse to be shaken.

domain_questions.question: How do you carry a heavy responsibility?

options.B: I break it into parts and manage each one.
options.C: I stay loose and handle it by feel as it evolves.
options.D: I pour myself into it completely until it's done.

options.F: I quietly handle it alone without making it anyone's business.
options.G: I hold the whole of it clear in my mind and bring others around to my view.
options.H: I carry it in the open for all to see, or bury it so deep only I feel the weight.

domain_questions.question: Which version of 'larger than life' actually appeals to you?

options.A: Being so in tune with everything that I never feel out of place.
options.B: Being the mystery no one can quite pin down.
options.C: Being the spark that lights up everyone around me.
options.D: Being the mind everyone defers to.
options.E: Being the one who holds steady when everything shakes.

options.H: Being the revelation that makes everyone reconsider what they thought they knew.

domain_questions.question: What's your favorite way to push toward a goal?

options.A: Let momentum build naturally and ride the wave.

domain_questions.question: When everything around you speeds up and gets chaotic, what do you rely on?

options.A: My intensity — I rise to meet the noise.

domain_questions.question: What does 'staying power' mean to you?

domain_questions.question: How do you most like to be underestimated?

options.A: Let them think I'm harmless and easy to read.
options.B: Let them swing at where I was, not where I am.
options.C: Let them forget I'm even in the room.
options.D: Let them miss that I've already arranged the outcome.

options.F: Let them ignore the pressure building in me until it's too late.
options.G: Let them plot in the open — I already know every move before they make it.
options.H: Let them think I'm obvious and simple — then show them I have depths they never imagined.

domain_questions.question: Which daydream of scale do you keep returning to?

options.A: Being so attuned I always know exactly what's needed.

options.C: Seeing the whole landscape and crossing it at will.
options.D: Standing tall enough to shield everyone behind me.

domain_questions.question: What's the most honest engine of your drive?

options.A: Forward grind — once committed I don't stop.

domain_questions.question: What's your relationship to your own strength?

options.A: It's loudest when I refuse to be silenced.

domain_questions.question: How do you outlast an opponent?

domain_questions.question: Which kind of dominance feels most satisfying?

domain_questions.question: What's your way of staying connected to the people you care about?

domain_questions.question: What's your truest form of momentum?

options.A: In passionate bursts, then more bursts.

domain_questions.question: What's the legacy-feeling you most want to leave?

flavor_questions.question: What do you trust most about your own body?

flavor_questions.question: How do you escape when things go wrong?

flavor_questions.question: What's your relationship to solid matter?

flavor_questions.question: What do you trust most when the situation turns?

options.B: That I can move unseen to where I need to be.
options.C: That I can punish anyone who gets too close.
options.D: That I can make something real and solid that stands firm.

flavor_questions.question: How do you recover after being pushed to your limit?

flavor_questions.question: What quietly satisfies you most about how you handle pressure?

flavor_questions.question: How would an old friend sum you up?

flavor_questions.question: What's your relationship to force?

flavor_questions.question: How do you protect your allies?

flavor_questions.question: What do you do when the enemy has you outnumbered?

flavor_questions.question: How do you shape the world around you?

options.A: I move things without touching them — pure will, made visible.

options.E: I duplicate until I am the terrain.
options.F: I redirect every force they throw until the world itself turns on them.

flavor_questions.question: What's your most natural response to danger?

options.A: I raise a shield — fast, absolute, and unyielding.
options.B: I split — one body draws the hit, another delivers the counter.
options.C: I heal through it. Pain is temporary; I am not.
options.D: I crush them under their own weight.
options.E: I turn their momentum into a weapon and throw it back.
options.F: I wrap myself in a shell of scrap metal and wait for the opening.

flavor_questions.question: What's your instinct in a fight?

options.A: I become the predator — fast, fierce, and final.
options.B: I let the environment do the work — roots, vines, and shifting earth.
options.C: I change my body until I'm armored, venomous, or untouchable.
options.D: I call down wind and rain and let nature finish it.
options.E: I release a fog of instinct — fear, lust, rage — and watch them unravel.
options.F: I become something they've never seen and don't know how to fight.

flavor_questions.question: How do you heal?

options.A: I don't — I adapt so I never need to.
options.B: I let the wild take the damage and grow back stronger.
options.C: I shift my flesh until the wound is gone.
options.D: I accelerate my own biology until I close the gap.
options.E: I change form entirely, leaving the damage behind.
options.F: I draw vitality from the living things around me.

flavor_questions.question: What's your relationship to change?

options.A: I am constant — the world changes around me.
options.B: I flow with it — seasons, cycles, and adaptation.
options.C: I force it — my body becomes whatever survival demands.
options.D: I wear it — a new face for every need.
options.E: I trigger it in others — a chemical push that makes them someone else.
options.F: I summon it — the weather turns because I will it.

flavor_questions.question: How do you mark your territory?

options.A: I reshape the land itself — roots, stone, and growth.
options.B: I leave a scent that warns others away.
options.C: I become the apex presence — no one challenges the top predator.
options.D: I make the air itself heavy with my will — storms, heat, and pressure.
options.E: I adapt until the territory is an extension of my body.
options.F: I shift between forms so no one knows what rules here.

flavor_questions.question: What do you trust most about your own nature?

options.A: That I can become anything I need to be.
options.B: That the wild answers my call.
options.C: That my body will find a way to survive.
options.D: That my presence changes the mood of every room.
options.E: That I can wear a thousand faces and none of them are truly me.
options.F: That the sky itself moves when I demand it.

flavor_questions.question: What's your relationship to truth?

options.A: I know it — I hear what they really think beneath the lie.
options.B: I control it — I make them see what I need them to see.
options.C: I destroy it — I hit their mind so hard that reality frays.
options.D: I protect it — I keep my own thoughts clear and sealed.
options.E: I bend it — I move the world without touching it, and who's to say what's real?
options.F: I uncover it — I feel their intent before they know it themselves.

flavor_questions.question: How do you handle a crowd?

options.A: I read the mood of the whole room at once.
options.B: I plant a single thought and watch it spread.
options.C: I shield myself from the noise — a wall of silence in a sea of sound.
options.D: I push them — one command, and they move like puppets.
options.E: I scatter their senses — illusions, whispers, and doubt.
options.F: I don't. Crowds are overwhelming; I stay at the edges.

flavor_questions.question: What's your preferred battlefield?

options.A: The mind — I fight where they can't see me.
options.B: The senses — I make them doubt everything they perceive.
options.C: The will — I break their desire to resist.
options.D: The physical world — I move objects, not people.
options.E: The hidden space — I read their plans before they make them.
options.F: The defended self — I let them tire themselves against my walls.

flavor_questions.question: How do you win a fight without throwing a punch?

options.A: I make them want to stop.
options.B: I show them a reality where they've already lost.
options.C: I hit their mind so hard they can't stand.
options.D: I read their next move and counter before they think it.
options.E: I turn their own weapons against them from across the room.
options.F: I wait — they exhaust themselves against my defenses.

flavor_questions.question: What do you fear most?

options.A: Having my own mind read.
options.B: Losing control of my own thoughts.
options.C: Being unable to tell what's real.
options.D: Being cut off — alone in a silent head.
options.E: Being overwhelmed by too many voices.
options.F: Being powerless — unable to move, unable to act.
